name: Orange Meringue Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Sugar, plus additional for topping
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour and salt with vegetable shortening in a large mixing bowl. Cut in the shortening until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
2. Stir in enough ice water to enable the dough to be gathered into a ball. Flatten the ball into a disk, wrap in waxed paper, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
3. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the pastry into an 11-inch round.
4. Fit the pastry into a 9-inch pan without stretching. Trim, leaving a 1/2-inch overhang all around; discard the pastry scraps or reserve for another purpose. Tuck under the excess pastry and crimp to make a decorative edge. Refrigerate for 30 minutes before baking.
5. Preheat the oven to 425°F. Line the pastry with aluminum foil and weigh down with pie weights or dried beans to prevent bubbling.
6. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until crisp and golden. Cool on a rack.
7. Prepare the filling: In a large mixing bowl, combine cornstarch, flour, sugar, and salt. Stir in boiling water constantly and blend until smooth. Add egg yolks and orange juice; beat until smooth. Transfer to a double boiler over (but not touching) boiling water and cook, stirring frequently, for 15-20 minutes. Remove from heat when very thick, add butter, orange zest, lemon juice, and grenadine; stir until butter melts. Let cool to room temperature.
8. Pour into the pre-baked pie shell, cover with waxed paper, and chill for at least 5 hours or overnight.
9. Prepare the meringue: Preheat oven to 350°F. Whisk egg whites until foamy, add salt and beat until foamy. Add cream of tartar and beat until soft peaks form. Gradually add sugar while beating until stiff and glossy. Do not overbeat; pile on filling, mounding in center and spreading out to seal.
10. Bake for 15 minutes or until golden. Let cool and chill for 3 hours before serving. To slice neatly, dip knife into boiling water before each cut.
